Description: Install the operator and required Kubernetes resources so it can reconcile Neo4j custom resources.
V1 Justification: Required to operate any Neo4j workload.

Description: Continuously reconcile declared custom resources and maintain the expected state.
Expose status, conditions, errors, and readiness on managed resources.
Support safe upgrade of the operator without disrupting managed Neo4j workloads.
Remove operator components while preserving managed data and workloads by default.
Create and manage the RBAC required for the selected operator scope.
V1 Justification: Different topologies require different RBAC models.

Description: Provide logs and metrics for operator health, reconciliation errors, and operational visibility.
Deploy a single Neo4j instance with configured edition, image, resources, storage, networking, and security.
Variant Groups: Neo4j Deployment Mode; Edition; Storage; Cloud Storage; Service Type; Port Combination; Security; Scheduling

Description: Deploy a Neo4j cluster with configurable members, discovery, services, and cluster readiness.
Variant Groups: Neo4j Deployment Mode; Cluster Size; Storage; Cloud Storage; Networking; Scheduling; Security
V1 Justification: Required for HA production use cases.

Description: Apply Neo4j configuration, JVM settings, environment variables, and optional APOC configuration.
V1 Justification: Required to cover current Helm chart configuration scope.

Description: Manage initial password, existing password secret, secret mounts, image pull secrets, and sensitive configuration.
Configure TLS for Bolt, HTTPS, and cluster communication, including cert mounts and reload behavior.
Provision or bind volumes for data and optional volumes for backups, logs, metrics, import, and licenses.
Configure internal and external services for Bolt, HTTP, HTTPS, backup, admin, and cluster internals.
V1 Justification: Required for applications and operations access.

Description: Configure node selectors, affinity, anti-affinity, tolerations, topology spread, priority class, service account, and graceful shutdown.
V1 Justification: Required for production placement and resilience.

Description: Configure startup, readiness, and liveness probes appropriate for Neo4j startup, recovery, and cluster formation.
V1 Justification: Required for safe Kubernetes lifecycle behavior.

Description: Detect configuration changes and apply them through controlled restarts or rolling updates.
Add or remove cluster members safely and enable servers when required.
Upgrade Neo4j image/version safely with validation and rollback considerations.
Support on-demand and scheduled backups with configurable backup storage and service exposure.
Restore Neo4j from a backup or seed URI, including optional secret mounts for cloud storage.
V1 Justification: Required for disaster recovery and environment cloning.

Description: Enable metrics and ServiceMonitor integration when Prometheus Operator is available.
Configure server logs, user logs, query logs, HTTP logs, security logs, and log storage.
Support offline maintenance mode and maintenance jobs such as dump/load, consistency checks, and backup verification.
V1 Justification: Advanced day-2 capability, can be phased after core lifecycle.

Description: Delete Neo4j Kubernetes resources while preserving PVCs by default and optionally cleaning up services/jobs/PVCs.
